chapter_title.ar: باب حَدِّ الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُغَسَّلُ بِهِ الْمَيِّتُ وَ الْكَافُورِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – A limit of the water to wash the deceased with, and (for) the camphor

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/323)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/323)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لِعَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) يَا عَلِيُّ إِذَا أَنَا مِتُّ فَغَسِّلْنِي بِسَبْعِ قِرَبٍ مِنْ بِئْرِ غَرْسٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said to Ali<sup>asws</sup>: ‘O Ali<sup>asws</sup>! When I<sup>saww</sup> pass away, so wash me<sup>saww</sup> with seven buckets from the well of Ghars’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Um ay r from Hafs al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah said to Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, “O Ali, when I die give me Ghusl (bath) with seven pails of water from Ghars well.’””
